exam_traps.trap: Landing/staging zone should use transient tables to avoid Fail-Safe costs
exam_traps.why_confusing: Raw/staging data that gets transformed and loaded into permanent tables doesn't need 7-day Fail-Safe. Using transient tables for staging saves storage costs. Permanent tables are for curated/production data.
exam_traps.correct_answer: Staging/landing = transient tables (no Fail-Safe). Production/curated = permanent tables.

questions.question: A team uses dbt to transform data in Snowflake. In the medallion architecture, where do dbt staging models and mart models typically map?

options.text: Staging models = bronze, mart models = silver.
Staging models = silver, mart models = gold.
Staging models = gold, mart models = bronze.
dbt does not follow medallion architecture patterns.

questions.overall_explanation: In dbt's convention: staging models clean and standardize raw data (silver/curated layer), and mart models create business-ready aggregations and fact/dimension tables (gold/consumption layer). The bronze layer is typically populated by ingestion tools (Snowpipe, COPY INTO) before dbt runs.
